schedule.description: STORY 1: Game 2 recap — Cubs 5, Reds 3 (Bregman HR)
STORY 2: Wild card race — 53-42, last game before break
STORY 3: Taillon update — 4⅔ scoreless at Iowa, return soon
STORY 4: PCA to Philly — leads all of baseball in WAR
STORY 5: Series finale preview — Boyd vs Abbott 12:40 PM CT

stories.title: Cubs 5, Reds 3 — Bregman's Go-Ahead Blast (Game 2 Recap)
stories.angle: NEW STORY — Alex Bregman's two-run HR in the 7th inning put Chicago ahead for good. Michael Busch went 3-for-4, Carson Kelly added a solo HR, and Trent Thornton nailed down his 3rd save. Cubs even the series at 1-1. Cubs now 53-42.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Bregman said enough of that. 💪

Two-run HR in the 7th put Chicago ahead for good. Cubs 5, Reds 3. Busch went 3-for-4, Kelly launched a solo shot, and Thornton slammed the door with save No. 3.

Cubs 53-42 heading to the series finale.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Wild Card Race — 53-42, Last Game Before the Break
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — Cubs 53-42, right in the thick of the NL Wild Card race. Today is the final regular-season game before the All-Star break. Win the series, head to Philly with PCA, come back July 17 with momentum.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
53-42. Wild Card race is tight — and today is the last Cubs game before the All-Star break.

Win the series finale. Send PCA to Philly with momentum. Come back July 17 at Wrigley looking like a team that belongs in October.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

stories.title: Taillon Dominant at Iowa — Return This Month
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — Taillon threw 4⅔ scoreless innings at Iowa on Saturday (second consecutive strong rehab start). Expected to return after the All-Star break, likely during the Twins series (July 17) or Tigers series (July 20-22). Rotation reinforcements are on the way.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Jameson Taillon: 4⅔ scoreless innings at Iowa on Saturday. One hit. One walk. Three strikeouts.

Two dominant rehab starts. He's expected back after the break — Twins series starts July 17 at Wrigley.

The rotation cavalry is assembling. 🔧

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Taillon Dominant at Iowa — Return This Month
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — Taillon covered July 5 (Iowa rehab start), July 6 (second rehab at South Bend correction), July 7-10 (ongoing timeline updates). Today: second consecutive strong Iowa outing with firmer return timeline.
**Key Facts:**
- Date: Saturday July 11, 2026 (yesterday) — Iowa Cubs
- Line: 4⅔ IP, 0 ER, 1 H, 1 BB, 3 K (HIGH — MLB.com + Bleed Cubbie Blue)
- Second consecutive rehab start (both scoreless) — HIGH
- Return target: post-All-Star break, Twins series (July 17-19) or Tigers series (July 20-22) — MEDIUM (MLB.com directional)
- Shaw: won't return before ASB (HIGH — Counsell confirmation)
**Sources:**
- MLB.com: https://www.mlb.com/cubs/news/jameson-taillon-makes-second-rehab-appearance
- Bleed Cubbie Blue minor league wrap: https://www.bleedcubbieblue.com/chicago-cubs-prospects/225278/
**Freshness:** Published July 11 (last night). Source age < 12 hours.
**Why it matters:** Cubs fans are eager for rotation reinforcements. Taillon's dominant showing is good news after months of IL stays.

stories.title: PCA to Philadelphia — The Best Player in Baseball Is a Cub
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP — Pete Crow-Armstrong leads all of baseball in WAR, heading to the All-Star Game on Tuesday (July 14, Citizens Bank Park, 8 PM ET on FOX). He's been the engine of this team through a pennant race with a rotation decimated by injuries. Statement tweet.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Pete Crow-Armstrong leads ALL of baseball in WAR — carrying this team through a pennant race on a rotation held together with duct tape and prayers.

Tuesday night. Citizens Bank Park. All-Star Game.

The best player in baseball is a Cub.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

research.facts:
PCA to Philadelphia — The Best Player in Baseball Is a Cub
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP — PCA All-Star covered multiple times (July 5: selected; July 10: 20/20 milestone + bold take; July 11: pre-game hype countdown). Today: final pre-break statement tweet on his MLB WAR leadership and All-Star appearance.
**Key Facts:**
- PCA leads all of baseball in WAR (HIGH — multiple sources, consistent with story history)
- WAR figure: ~5.2 per MLB.com article; ~5.8 per July 10 pipeline (FanGraphs vs Baseball Reference; used without number in tweet to avoid false precision — verified claim is "leads all baseball")
- All-Star Game: Tuesday July 14, 8 PM ET on FOX, Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia (HIGH — multiple sources)
- PCA is Cubs' lone All-Star (HIGH — confirmed via multiple sources, story history)
- July stats: 8-for-17 (.471) in 5 games (MEDIUM — single search source)
- July 4 PCA season stats context: .289 BA, 19 HR as of ML source (MEDIUM; story history shows 21 HR as of July 10 — search may lag)
**Sources:**
- MLB.com All-Star: https://www.mlb.com/news/pete-crow-armstrong-earns-second-straight-all-star-nod-for-cubs
- Bleed Cubbie Blue
- Chicago Sun-Times
**Note on WAR:** Two sources show 5.2 (MLB.com All-Star article) and prior pipeline shows 5.8 (July 10). Discrepancy likely FanGraphs vs Baseball Reference. Used "leads all of baseball in WAR" without citing a specific figure to avoid false precision.
**Why it matters:** PCA is the emotional centerpiece of this Cubs team. The All-Star Game is Tuesday. This tweet closes the loop before the break.

stories.title: Series Finale Preview — Boyd vs Abbott, 12:40 PM CT
stories.angle: NEW STORY — Series tied 1-1. Matthew Boyd takes the ball for Chicago against Andrew Abbott and the Reds. Win today, take the series, head into the break with all the momentum.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Series finale. Great American Ball Park. 12:40 PM CT.

Matthew Boyd takes the ball against Andrew Abbott and the Reds. Split the first two. One game left.

Cubs (53-42) can take this series and head into the All-Star break with all the momentum.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
